tasks.description: Add origin-ref domain types to libs/domain/src/tddd/semantic_verify.rs. (1) Add SpecSectionKind enum with five variants: Goal, InScope, OutOfScope, Constraint, AcceptanceCriteria. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. Module path: domain::tddd::semantic_verify. (2) Add CatalogueSectionKey enum with three variants: Types, Traits, Functions. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (3) Add CatalogueEntryKey newtype struct wrapping String with a non-empty invariant; impl try_new(raw: String) -> Result<CatalogueEntryKey, ValidationError> rejecting empty or whitespace-only strings; impl as_str(&self) -> &str. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (4) Add SpecElementRef struct with fields section: SpecSectionKind, element_id: SpecElementId, text_label: String; impl new(section, element_id, text_label) -> Self. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (5) Add AdrDecisionRef struct with fields file_path: String, decision_id: String; impl new(file_path, decision_id) -> Self. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (6) Add CatalogueEntryRef struct with fields file_path: String, section_key: CatalogueSectionKey, entry_key: CatalogueEntryKey; impl new(file_path, section_key, entry_key) -> Self. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (7) Add VerifyOriginRef enum with three variants: SpecElement(SpecElementRef), AdrDecision(AdrDecisionRef), CatalogueEntry(CatalogueEntryRef). Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (8) Re-export all new types from domain::tddd::semantic_verify. (9) Add unit tests for CatalogueEntryKey::try_new (valid, empty, whitespace-only), VerifyOriginRef construction for each variant. This task is purely additive: no existing types or function signatures are modified. Catalogue refs: domain-types.json SpecSectionKind, CatalogueSectionKey, CatalogueEntryKey, SpecElementRef, AdrDecisionRef, CatalogueEntryRef, VerifyOriginRef. Spec refs: IN-07, AC-11.

Cross-layer schema migration: add claim_origin/evidence_origin to SemanticVerifyEntry and RefVerifyPair, update pair sources to compute real origins, update codec and all callers. Depends on: T001. This is an atomic change spanning domain, usecase, and infrastructure to keep all layers compilable. (1) Domain (libs/domain/src/tddd/semantic_verify.rs): add claim_origin: VerifyOriginRef and evidence_origin: VerifyOriginRef fields to SemanticVerifyEntry; update SemanticVerifyEntry::new() to a 5-parameter signature: new(claim_hash: ContentHash, evidence_hash: ContentHash, verdict: SemanticVerdict, claim_origin: VerifyOriginRef, evidence_origin: VerifyOriginRef) -> Self; update domain unit tests accordingly. (2) Usecase (libs/usecase/src/ref_verify.rs): add claim_origin: VerifyOriginRef and evidence_origin: VerifyOriginRef fields to RefVerifyPair; update make_known_bad_probe in infrastructure to supply synthetic origins (VerifyOriginRef::SpecElement with placeholder SpecSectionKind::Goal, a synthetic SpecElementId, and probe index as text_label). (3) Infrastructure Chain-1 pair source (libs/infrastructure/src/ref_verify/pair_source.rs): in enumerate_chain1_pairs, compute claim_origin as VerifyOriginRef::SpecElement(SpecElementRef::new(map_section_str_to_kind(section), SpecElementId::try_new(id.to_owned())?, req.text().to_owned())) where map_section_str_to_kind maps 'goal'->'Goal', 'in_scope'->'InScope', 'out_of_scope'->'OutOfScope', 'constraint'->'Constraint', 'acceptance_criteria'->'AcceptanceCriteria'; compute evidence_origin as VerifyOriginRef::AdrDecision(AdrDecisionRef::new(adr_ref.file.to_string_lossy().into_owned(), adr_ref.anchor.as_ref().to_owned())); set RefVerifyPair::claim_origin and evidence_origin accordingly. (4) Infrastructure Chain-2 pair source (libs/infrastructure/src/ref_verify/pair_source_chain2.rs): in enumerate_chain2_pairs_for_catalogue, compute claim_origin as VerifyOriginRef::CatalogueEntry(CatalogueEntryRef::new(catalogue_path relative to project_root as string, map_section_str_to_catalogue_key(section), CatalogueEntryKey::try_new(entry_key.as_str().to_owned())?)) where map_section_str_to_catalogue_key maps 'types'->'Types', 'traits'->'Traits', 'functions'->'Functions'; compute evidence_origin as VerifyOriginRef::SpecElement(SpecElementRef::new(find_spec_element_section_kind(spec_doc, anchor)?, SpecElementId::try_new(anchor.to_owned())?, find_spec_element_text(spec_doc, anchor)?)) with helpers find_spec_element_section_kind and find_spec_element_text returning errors when the referenced spec element section or text is missing; set RefVerifyPair::claim_origin and evidence_origin accordingly. (5) Infrastructure codec (libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/semantic_verify_codec.rs): add DTO types SpecSectionKindDto (serde enum, snake_case rename, deny_unknown_fields), CatalogueSectionKeyDto (serde enum, deny_unknown_fields), SpecElementRefDto (serde struct, deny_unknown_fields with fields section: SpecSectionKindDto, element_id: String, text_label: String), AdrDecisionRefDto (serde struct, deny_unknown_fields with fields file_path: String, decision_id: String), CatalogueEntryRefDto (serde struct, deny_unknown_fields with fields file_path: String, section_key: CatalogueSectionKeyDto, entry_key: String), VerifyOriginRefDto (adjacently tagged serde enum with tag='kind', content='data', deny_unknown_fields, three variants); add conversion functions origin_to_dto and origin_from_dto; add claim_origin: VerifyOriginRefDto and evidence_origin: VerifyOriginRefDto fields to SemanticVerifyEntryDto (required, no serde(default)); update entry_to_dto and entry_from_dto to include origin fields; add codec unit tests for round-trip of all VerifyOriginRef variants, and a test that JSON entries without claim_origin or evidence_origin fail with SemanticVerifyCodecError::Json (missing field). (6) Usecase interactor (libs/usecase/src/ref_verify/interactor.rs): update the SemanticVerifyEntry::new() call to pass pair.claim_origin.clone() and pair.evidence_origin.clone(); when loading an existing cache for run, do not silently deserialize old-schema entries as valid cache. Detect the codec's missing-origin old-schema error, treat that scope as an invalidated empty cache for this run, and overwrite it with newly verified entries in the new schema; all other cache load errors still propagate. (7) cli_composition test fixtures (apps/cli-composition/src/ref_verify.rs): update write_cache_for_first_chain1_pair to construct SemanticVerifyEntry::new() with 5 arguments; use VerifyOriginRef::SpecElement(SpecElementRef::new(SpecSectionKind::Goal, SpecElementId::try_new('GO-01'.to_owned()).unwrap(), 'test claim'.to_owned())) as claim_origin and VerifyOriginRef::AdrDecision(AdrDecisionRef::new('knowledge/adr/decision.md'.to_owned(), 'D1'.to_owned())) as evidence_origin for the Chain-1 fixture. Infrastructure codec tests updated similarly with synthetic origins. Catalogue refs: domain-types.json SemanticVerifyEntry, usecase-types.json RefVerifyPair, infrastructure-types.json SemanticVerifyEntryDto / SpecSectionKindDto / CatalogueSectionKeyDto / SpecElementRefDto / AdrDecisionRefDto / CatalogueEntryRefDto / VerifyOriginRefDto. Spec refs: IN-04, IN-07, IN-08, AC-03, AC-11, AC-12, CN-05.

Add results filter types, output DTO types, and extend RefVerifyAggregateService in the usecase layer. Depends on: T001. (1) Add RefVerifyChainFilter enum in libs/usecase/src/ref_verify/driver_service.rs with three variants: Chain1, Chain2, All. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (2) Add RefVerifyLayerFilter enum with two variants: Specific(domain::tddd::LayerId), All. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (3) Add RefVerifyVerdictFilter enum with five variants: FailPending, Pass, Fail, Pending, All. FailPending is the default-output filter for omitted CLI --filter and includes fail plus pending records; explicit CLI values map to Pass/Fail/Pending/All. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq. (4) Add RefVerifyLaneSummary struct (Dto role, no serde) with fields: label: String, pass_count: usize, fail_count: usize, pending_count: usize. Derives: Debug, Clone. (5) Add RefVerifyPairRecord struct (Dto role, no serde) with fields: chain_scope: RefVerifyCacheScope, chain_layer: String, claim_hash: domain::plan_ref::ContentHash, evidence_hash: domain::plan_ref::ContentHash, verdict: domain::tddd::semantic_verify::SemanticVerdict, reason: String, claim_origin: domain::tddd::semantic_verify::VerifyOriginRef, evidence_origin: domain::tddd::semantic_verify::VerifyOriginRef. Derives: Debug, Clone. (6) Add RefVerifyResultsOutput struct (Dto role, no serde) with fields: lane_summaries: Vec<RefVerifyLaneSummary>, pair_records: Vec<RefVerifyPairRecord>, total_pass: usize, total_fail: usize, total_pending: usize. Derives: Debug. (7) Add results method to RefVerifyAggregateService trait: fn results(&self, track_id: &str, items_dir: &Path, chain: RefVerifyChainFilter, layer: RefVerifyLayerFilter, verdict: RefVerifyVerdictFilter) -> Result<RefVerifyResultsOutput, RefVerifyDriverError>. Give the method a compile-safe default body returning Err(RefVerifyDriverError::Unavailable("ref-verify results not implemented".to_owned())) so existing implementors continue to compile in T003; T004 replaces this with the concrete FsRefVerifyAggregateAdapter implementation. Add a blanket doc comment on the trait that references the signature and default behavior from the catalogue. Module path: usecase::ref_verify::driver_service. Catalogue refs: usecase-types.json RefVerifyChainFilter, RefVerifyLayerFilter, RefVerifyVerdictFilter, RefVerifyLaneSummary, RefVerifyPairRecord, RefVerifyResultsOutput, RefVerifyAggregateService. Spec refs: IN-01, IN-02, IN-03, IN-06, AC-02, AC-05, AC-07, AC-08, AC-09.

Implement FsRefVerifyAggregateAdapter::results in the infrastructure layer. Depends on: T002, T003. (1) Implement the results method on FsRefVerifyAggregateAdapter in libs/infrastructure/src/ref_verify/driver_adapter.rs. The implementation must: (a) resolve project_root and validate track_id (reuse existing resolve_project_root + validate_track_id helpers), (b) resolve scope using RefVerifyScopeResolver (existence-based, same as run/check_approved), (c) load all cache files: for Chain-1 (SpecAdr scope), call RefVerifyCacheAdapter::load_entries; for each TDDD layer (from architecture-rules.json dynamic resolution via RefVerifyScopeResolver or TdddLayerBinding enumeration), call load_entries for CatalogueSpec scope; reject old-schema cache files (the codec already does this via missing-field parse error), (d) load current pair source read-only: call RefVerifyPairSourceAdapter to enumerate ALL current pairs without invoking any LLM verifier; build a lookup map from (claim_hash, evidence_hash) to VerifyOriginRef pair for pending-pair origin derivation, (e) classify each current pair: pass = cache entry with SemanticVerdict::Pass, fail = cache entry with SemanticVerdict::Fail, pending = no pass/fail cache entry for this pair's hashes (includes cache miss AND SemanticVerdict::Pending entries treated as unresolved); for pending pairs, use origins from the pair source lookup and set reason to an unresolved-pair message; for pass/fail pairs, use origins from the cache entry and set reason from SemanticVerdict::Fail (empty for Pass), (f) apply chain and layer filters: if chain=Chain1, include only SpecAdr pairs; if chain=Chain2, include only CatalogueSpec pairs; if chain=All, include all; if layer=Specific(name), filter Chain2 pairs to that layer; layer=All includes all; --chain 1 with layer filter is a no-op (Chain1 has no layer dimension), (g) apply verdict filter to build pair_records, setting chain_layer to 'Chain1' or 'Chain2:<layer_name>': fail_pending=Fail or Pending pairs, pass=only Pass pairs, fail=only Fail pairs, pending=only Pending pairs, all=all; the lane_summaries and total counts always use all verdicts for the selected chain/layer lanes regardless of verdict filter, (h) build RefVerifyLaneSummary per lane: Chain1 produces one lane labeled 'Chain1 (spec↔ADR)'; Chain2 produces one lane per TDDD layer labeled 'Chain2:<layer_name>'; layer filter is applied to lane list; chain filter is applied to include/exclude Chain1 or Chain2 lanes, (i) assemble RefVerifyResultsOutput: lane_summaries (filtered by chain + layer), pair_records (filtered by chain + layer + verdict), total_pass/fail/pending as totals across the chain/layer-filtered lane set, not narrowed by verdict filter. (2) Update FsRefVerifyAggregateAdapter impl usecase::ref_verify::RefVerifyAggregateService to include the results method. (3) Add unit tests covering: empty cache returns all-pending result, pass entry returns pass lane summary, layer filter narrows Chain2 output, chain filter excludes Chain1 or Chain2 lanes, verdict filter fail_pending/pass/fail/pending/all each return correct subsets, lane summary totals are unaffected by verdict filter. Catalogue refs: infrastructure-types.json FsRefVerifyAggregateAdapter. Spec refs: IN-01, IN-02, IN-03, IN-04, IN-05, IN-06, AC-02, AC-03, AC-04, AC-05, AC-07, AC-08, AC-09, CN-04, CN-06.

Add the results subcommand to the CLI layer and wire dispatch. Depends on: T005. (1) Add RefVerifyChainArg clap ValueEnum in apps/cli/src/commands/ref_verify.rs with variants: Chain1 (#[value(name = '1')]), Chain2 (#[value(name = '2')]), All (#[value(name = 'all')]). Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, clap::ValueEnum. Module path: cli::commands::ref_verify. (2) Add RefVerifyVerdictFilterArg clap ValueEnum with variants: Pass, Fail, Pending, All using automatic kebab-case. Derives: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, clap::ValueEnum. (3) Add RefVerifyResultsArgs struct deriving Debug, clap::Args with fields: track_id: Option<String> (--track-id, optional, auto-resolved from branch when absent, documented in help text), items_dir: PathBuf (--items-dir, default='track/items'), chain: RefVerifyChainArg (#[arg(long, default_value = 'all')]), layer: String (#[arg(long, default_value = 'all')], valid layer names from architecture-rules.json at runtime), filter: Option<RefVerifyVerdictFilterArg> (#[arg(long = 'filter')], no clap default; omitted filter maps to RefVerifyVerdictSelect::FailPending); include help text on --layer documenting that valid layer names are resolved dynamically and 'all' selects all layers. (4) Add Results(RefVerifyResultsArgs) variant to RefVerifyCommand enum; update derive to include the new variant in the Subcommand dispatch. (5) Add execute_results private function: call crate::commands::track::resolve_track_id(args.track_id.clone(), &args.items_dir) to auto-resolve track_id (using existing pattern from execute_run / execute_check_approved); convert RefVerifyChainArg to cli_driver::ref_verify::RefVerifyChainSelect; convert Option<RefVerifyVerdictFilterArg> to cli_driver::ref_verify::RefVerifyVerdictSelect, mapping None to FailPending and Some(pass/fail/pending/all) to the explicit variant; pass args.layer.clone() as the layer string; construct RefVerifyResultsInput and call RefVerifyCompositionRoot::new().ref_verify_driver().handle(RefVerifyInput::Results(...)). (6) Wire execute_results in the execute() dispatch match. (7) Add clap arg-parsing tests: results with no args uses defaults (chain=all, filter=None -> FailPending, layer='all', track_id=None), explicit --chain 1 parses to Chain1, explicit --filter pass parses to Some(Pass), explicit --filter all parses to Some(All), explicit --layer domain stores 'domain', explicit --track-id my-track stores Some('my-track'), unknown --format flag is rejected by clap. Catalogue refs: cli-types.json RefVerifyChainArg, RefVerifyVerdictFilterArg, RefVerifyResultsArgs, RefVerifyCommand, cli::commands::ref_verify::execute. Spec refs: IN-01, AC-01, AC-06, AC-07, AC-08, AC-09, AC-10, CN-01, CN-02, CN-03.

Add cli_composition wiring for ref_verify_results and add integration test. Depends on: T004, T006. (1) Add RefVerifyResultsInput struct in apps/cli-composition/src/ref_verify.rs with fields: track_id: String, items_dir: std::path::PathBuf, chain: usecase::ref_verify::RefVerifyChainFilter, layer: usecase::ref_verify::RefVerifyLayerFilter, verdict: usecase::ref_verify::RefVerifyVerdictFilter. Derives: Debug, Clone. Module path: cli_composition::ref_verify. (2) Add ref_verify_results(&self, input: RefVerifyResultsInput) -> Result<CommandOutcome, CompositionError> method to RefVerifyCompositionRoot: delegate to self.ref_verify_driver().handle(cli_driver::ref_verify::RefVerifyInput::Results(cli_driver::ref_verify::RefVerifyResultsInput { track_id: input.track_id, items_dir: input.items_dir, chain: convert_chain_filter(input.chain), layer: layer_filter_to_string(input.layer), verdict: convert_verdict_filter(input.verdict) })).map_err_to_composition_error() where convert_chain_filter forwards Chain1/Chain2/All, layer_filter_to_string maps RefVerifyLayerFilter::All to 'all' and Specific(id) to id.as_ref().to_owned(), and convert_verdict_filter forwards FailPending/Pass/Fail/Pending/All. (3) Re-export RefVerifyResultsInput from apps/cli-composition/src/lib.rs alongside the existing RefVerifyRunInput and RefVerifyCheckApprovedInput re-exports. (4) Add a cfg(test) integration test in apps/cli-composition/src/ref_verify.rs: test_ref_verify_results_no_cache_returns_all_pending: using the existing write_chain1_fixture helper to set up a track, call RefVerifyCompositionRoot::new().ref_verify_results(RefVerifyResultsInput { track_id, items_dir, chain: RefVerifyChainFilter::All, layer: RefVerifyLayerFilter::All, verdict: RefVerifyVerdictFilter::FailPending }) inside with_fake_track_branch; assert outcome.exit_code == 0; assert stdout contains 'Summary:'; assert total_pass and total_fail are 0 (derived from stdout parsing or CommandOutcome structure). Catalogue refs: cli_composition-types.json RefVerifyResultsInput, RefVerifyCompositionRoot. Spec refs: IN-01, AC-01, AC-06, AC-07, AC-08, AC-09, CN-03.

Update /track:ref-verify skill Step 2 to use sotp ref-verify results instead of direct verify-cache reads. Depends on: T006. (1) In .claude/commands/track/ref-verify.md, update the Step 2 section (currently titled 'Interpret the outcome'): replace the instruction to read spec-adr-verify-cache.json / <layer>-catalogue-spec-verify-cache.json directly with the instruction to run 'bin/sotp ref-verify results' and interpret its output. The BLOCKED outcome section currently says: 'Identify the failing pair(s) from the relevant verify-cache artifact (spec-adr-verify-cache.json for Chain①, <layer>-catalogue-spec-verify-cache.json for Chain②) and route the fix to the owning writer'. Replace this with: 'Run bin/sotp ref-verify results to identify failing and pending pairs. Each fail record shows the claim_origin (which artifact made the claim) and evidence_origin (which artifact provided the evidence), enabling routing to the correct writer (spec-designer for spec text, type-designer for catalogue entries, adr-editor for ADR decisions) without reading the verify-cache JSON files directly'. (2) Ensure the updated Step 2 does not instruct the reader to open or read any verify-cache JSON file directly. (3) No code changes in this task; only .claude/commands/track/ref-verify.md is modified. Catalogue refs: none (skill file, not a catalogue entry). Spec refs: IN-09, AC-13.

summary: 8-task structure organized into four layers: (A) domain origin-ref types (T001), (B) cross-layer schema migration (T002), (C) usecase results port types (T003), (D) infrastructure results adapter (T004), (E) cli_driver dispatch (T005), (F) CLI subcommand (T006), (G) cli_composition wiring + integration test (T007), (H) skill update (T008).

T001 is purely additive (no breaking changes): adds 7 new domain value types that form the origin-reference vocabulary. T002 is the schema-migration task and must be committed atomically because modifying SemanticVerifyEntry and RefVerifyPair breaks all callers across domain, usecase, and infrastructure simultaneously. T002 is the largest task (~400 lines) but cannot be safely split without intermediate broken builds.

T003 and T004 follow the hexagonal order: usecase port definition first, then infrastructure adapter. T003 depends on T001 (VerifyOriginRef must exist before RefVerifyPairRecord can reference it); T004 depends on both T002 (cache schema) and T003 (trait method to implement).
T005, T006, T007 implement the three outward-facing layers (cli_driver, cli, cli_composition) in inward-to-outward order. T008 is a documentation-only task that updates the /track:ref-verify skill.

Task size targets: T001 ~120 lines, T002 ~400 lines (cross-layer atomic change), T003 ~130 lines, T004 ~250 lines, T005 ~180 lines, T006 ~170 lines, T007 ~120 lines, T008 ~25 lines. All except T002 are well within the 500-line reviewability guideline; T002 is justified by the atomic cross-layer constraint.

sections.title: Domain: Origin-Ref Type Vocabulary (T001)
description: IN-07 / AC-11: introduce the seven new domain value types in domain::tddd::semantic_verify that form the origin-reference vocabulary for the cache schema extension.

SpecSectionKind and CatalogueSectionKey are unit-variant enums encoding the two artifact taxonomies. CatalogueEntryKey is a non-empty validated newtype. SpecElementRef, AdrDecisionRef, and CatalogueEntryRef are typed record structs carrying the concrete position (section, id/key, path). VerifyOriginRef is the tagged-union type wrapping all three.

Purely additive: no existing types are modified, all current callers continue to compile unchanged. All new types are placed in domain::tddd::semantic_verify alongside SemanticVerifyEntry (same module).
Unit tests cover CatalogueEntryKey invariant enforcement and all three VerifyOriginRef variant constructions.

sections.title: Cross-Layer Schema Migration: SemanticVerifyEntry + RefVerifyPair + Codec (T002)
description: IN-07 / IN-08 / IN-04 / AC-11 / AC-12 / CN-05: atomic schema migration that adds claim_origin and evidence_origin to SemanticVerifyEntry (domain), RefVerifyPair (usecase), and SemanticVerifyEntryDto (infrastructure codec). All callers are updated in the same commit to keep every layer compilable.

Chain-1 pair source (pair_source.rs): SpecElementRef is constructed from the section label string (mapped to SpecSectionKind), the requirement id (validated as SpecElementId), and the requirement text. AdrDecisionRef is constructed from the ADR file path and anchor string. Both are stored in the RefVerifyPair.

Chain-2 pair source (pair_source_chain2.rs): CatalogueEntryRef is constructed from the catalogue file path, section key (types/traits/functions mapped to CatalogueSectionKey), and entry key (validated as CatalogueEntryKey). SpecElementRef for the evidence is derived by finding the spec element's section in the parsed SpecDocument. Both are stored in the RefVerifyPair.

Infrastructure codec (semantic_verify_codec.rs): new origin DTO types (SpecSectionKindDto, CatalogueSectionKeyDto, SpecElementRefDto, AdrDecisionRefDto, CatalogueEntryRefDto, VerifyOriginRefDto with adjacently-tagged serde) are added. SemanticVerifyEntryDto is catalogued with required claim_origin and evidence_origin fields; implementation updates the codec's cache-entry DTO shape in place rather than introducing a parallel DTO. No serde(default) is used, so old cache files without these fields fail with a missing-field JSON error, satisfying IN-08/CN-05. Schema version remains 1; rejection is via serde missing-field rather than a version bump.

Interactor (interactor.rs): SemanticVerifyEntry::new() call updated to pass pair.claim_origin.clone() and pair.evidence_origin.clone(), so all newly-verified pairs are written to cache with correct origin metadata. The run path treats the codec's missing-origin old-schema error as invalidated cache for that scope and overwrites it with newly verified new-schema entries; read-only consumers still fail closed on the same old-schema parse error.

Existing integration test fixtures in cli_composition: write_cache_for_first_chain1_pair updated to provide synthetic but valid origins (SpecElement for Chain-1 claim, AdrDecision for Chain-1 evidence).

sections.title: Usecase: Results Filter Types and RefVerifyAggregateService Extension (T003)
description: IN-01 / IN-02 / IN-03 / IN-06 / AC-07 / AC-08 / AC-09: add the usecase-layer filter enums (RefVerifyChainFilter, RefVerifyLayerFilter, RefVerifyVerdictFilter with FailPending/Pass/Fail/Pending/All) and output DTO structs (RefVerifyLaneSummary, RefVerifyPairRecord, RefVerifyResultsOutput) to driver_service.rs.
RefVerifyLayerFilter::Specific(LayerId) carries the resolved layer id (not a raw string) so the infrastructure adapter receives a typed value.

RefVerifyAggregateService trait gains a results() method signature with a temporary default body returning RefVerifyDriverError::Unavailable, so existing implementors compile after T003. The concrete implementation is in T004; this task defines the interface, associated types, and compile-safe default that the cli_driver (T005) depends on.
No filesystem I/O or serde in this layer (hexagonal boundary).

sections.title: Infrastructure: FsRefVerifyAggregateAdapter::results Implementation (T004)
description: IN-02 / IN-03 / IN-04 / IN-05 / IN-06 / AC-02 / AC-03 / AC-04 / AC-05 / AC-07 / AC-08 / AC-09 / CN-04 / CN-06: implement the results method on FsRefVerifyAggregateAdapter.

Existence-based scope resolution (same as run/check-approved) determines which chains and layers are active. Cache files are loaded read-only; old-schema cache (missing claim_origin/evidence_origin) fails with SemanticVerifyCodecError::Json, propagated as RefVerifyDriverError::Usecase.

Pair source is called read-only (no LLM subprocess) to enumerate current pairs and their origins. Pending pairs are identified as current pairs not matched by any Pass or Fail cache entry (including pairs with SemanticVerdict::Pending cache entries, which are treated as unresolved).

Chain and layer filters are applied to both lane_summaries and pair_records. Verdict filter applies only to pair_records; FailPending includes fail plus pending records, while Pass/Fail/Pending/All are the explicit --filter modes. Lane summary totals and Summary totals use all verdicts for the selected chain/layer lanes. The valid layer names come from the architecture-rules.json TDDD layer enumeration (dynamic resolution, not hardcoded).
No writer routing or LLM invocation in the results path (CN-06).

sections.title: CLI Driver: Results Input Types and Driver Dispatch (T005)
description: IN-01 / IN-02 / IN-03 / IN-06 / AC-05 / AC-10 / CN-01 / CN-02 / CN-04: add cli_driver-layer types and extend RefVerifyDriver.

RefVerifyChainSelect and RefVerifyVerdictSelect are cli_driver-level mirrors of the usecase filter enums, allowing the cli layer to construct RefVerifyResultsInput without importing usecase directly. RefVerifyVerdictSelect::FailPending represents omitted CLI --filter.

Layer is kept as a plain String at the cli_driver layer because valid layer names are dynamic (resolved from architecture-rules.json at runtime in the infrastructure layer); the infrastructure adapter performs LayerId validation.

Output formatting: the results text is assembled by the driver (not the infrastructure adapter), keeping the infrastructure adapter free of presentation concerns. Exit code is always 0 (CN-02) regardless of verdict content; non-zero exit is only returned for wiring/IO errors.

sections.title: CLI: results Subcommand Args and Dispatch (T006)
description: IN-01 / AC-01 / AC-06 / AC-07 / AC-08 / AC-09 / AC-10 / CN-01 / CN-03: add the RefVerifyResultsArgs struct and Results variant to RefVerifyCommand.
RefVerifyChainArg uses explicit #[value(name)] attributes to map variant names to the CLI values '1', '2', 'all', preserving the numeric convention from the ADR.

The --layer option is a plain String with default 'all'; the help text documents that valid layer names are resolved at runtime from architecture-rules.json. This avoids a clap ValueEnum that would hardcode layer names.

Track ID auto-resolution reuses the existing resolve_track_id helper from commands::track (same pattern as run and check-approved). Clap arg-parsing tests cover defaults (filter None -> FailPending), explicit values, and rejection of removed flags.

sections.title: CLI Composition: ref_verify_results Wiring and Integration Test (T007)
description: IN-01 / AC-01 / AC-06 / CN-03: add RefVerifyResultsInput and ref_verify_results to RefVerifyCompositionRoot.

The conversion from RefVerifyLayerFilter to the cli_driver-level String representation (layer_filter_to_string) happens at the cli_composition boundary: All maps to 'all', Specific(id) maps to the LayerId string.
Integration test: uses write_chain1_fixture to set up a track, calls ref_verify_results with All filters inside with_fake_track_branch, asserts exit_code=0 and that stdout contains the Summary line.

sections.title: Skill: Update /track:ref-verify Step 2 (T008)
description: IN-09 / AC-13: documentation-only commit updating .claude/commands/track/ref-verify.md.

The BLOCKED outcome section's instruction to manually read verify-cache JSON files is replaced with the instruction to run bin/sotp ref-verify results and read the claim_origin / evidence_origin fields for writer routing.
No Rust source changes. This task depends on T006 being done so that the sotp ref-verify results command is already in place when the skill references it.
